title: pariti wrayae
intro: Pariti wrayae, commonly known as "Java plum" or "Jambolan", is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia and Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to lower blood sugar levels and provide relief from fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pariti wrayae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pariti wrayae include improved blood sugar control, reduced fever, and relief from digestive issues, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
side_effects: Pariti wrayae can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain due to its high levels of alkaloids. In severe cases, overconsumption of the plant's extracts can lead to heart palpitations, seizures, and even coma. Other side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing, particularly for individuals with pre-existing conditions. Furthermore, using Pariti wrayae improperly can also interact negatively with certain medications, exacerbating their side effects and potentially leading to life-threatening complications.
